genre_refs: Henry Mosler 'Birth of the Flag' (American history painting / Betsy Ross legend)
description: A reproduction of the 19th/early-20th-century academic oil painting 'Birth of the Flag' by Henry Mosler, posted by the U.S. Department of Labor with no added on-image text (the framing message is supplied entirely in the tweet). The richly colored, warmly lit interior scene depicts four women in colonial-era dress in a paneled parlor with a brick fireplace and a curtained window. A standing woman in a yellow gown and white bonnet holds up a blue canton studded with white stars (an early American flag), while three seated women in green, blue, and salmon-orange dresses stitch and arrange the red-and-white striped field of the flag spread across their laps. The style is realist, painterly, sentimental Americana evoking the Betsy Ross flag-making legend. The Labor Department uses the fine-art image to wrap its 'America First / One American Heritage' nationalist messaging in venerated patriotic history, quoting George Washington's 1796 line about the 'just pride of Patriotism' in the caption. The apparent intent is to associate the administration's nationalism with founding-era reverence and national heritage; the target audience is patriotic, traditionalist Americans.
notable_text: (No on-image text. Caption attributes the work: 'Birth of the Flag' - Henry Mosler; 'One American Heritage'; George Washington 1796 quote)
